heading: Research directions
intro: Controlling fluorescence across space and time to reveal biological structure and heterogeneity.

directions.title: Fluorescence Modulation
directions.description: Tune fluorescence emission using chemical and physical methods.

directions.title: Multiplex Imaging
directions.description: High-throughput and large-field-of-view imaging of biological samples.

highlights.title: Electrochemically Controlled STORM
highlights.description: Electrochemical control of fluorophore blinking enables quantitative STORM imaging with tunable switching kinetics, reduced emitter overlap, and improved molecular counting.
highlights.citation: Y. Yang et al., Electrochemically controlled blinking of fluorophores for quantitative STORM imaging, Nature Photonics 18, 713–720 (2024).
highlights.image_alt: Two-color STORM image showing Alexa 647-labelled structures in green and CF680-labelled filament networks in magenta

highlights.title: Electrochemically Controlled SOFI
highlights.description: Electrochemical modulation accelerates and homogenizes dye switching, enabling faster and large-area super-resolution optical fluctuation imaging.
highlights.citation: Y. Yang et al., Electrochemically controlled switching of dyes for enhanced superresolution optical fluctuation imaging (EC-SOFI), PNAS 122, e2425390122 (2025).
highlights.image_alt: Sixth-order EC-SOFI grayscale image showing a dense microtubule filament network

highlights.title: Multiplex Imaging
highlights.description: Electrochemical fluorescence response profiles distinguish spectrally overlapping fluorophores, expanding the multiplexing capability of conventional fluorescence and STED microscopy.
highlights.citation: Y. Yang et al., Electrochemical fluorescence modulation enables simultaneous multicolour imaging, Nature Photonics 19, 718–724 (2025).
highlights.image_alt: Four-color fluorescence image of a cell showing paxillin in magenta, microtubules in yellow, actin in blue, and DNA in red
